Puls..mental_state: Extremely excitable and imaginative, prone to religious fanaticism, fixed ideas about Scripture, and irrational aversions to things well-established as normal (marriage, certain foods, social contact). In advanced states, the patient becomes taciturn and withdrawn, sitting silently day after day, answering only with nods or monosyllables.
Puls..emotional_pattern: Fundamentally tearful, sad, and despondent, with weeping that comes without cause and a deep melancholy that can progress to religious despair. The patient always feels slighted or fears being slighted, yet is ameliorated by walking in cool open air and worsened in the evening, with emotional symptoms closely intertwined with menstrual and uterine disorders.

Pyrog..personality: A restless, driven individual who cannot remain still and feels compelled to keep moving. They are loquacious and mentally accelerated, thinking and talking faster than usual, with a nervous energy that makes sitting intolerable.
Pyrog..mental_state: Marked confusion about bodily boundaries and personal identity — they may feel dispersed, as if covering the whole bed, or sense they become a different person when changing position. Delirium and mental fog alternate with periods of rapid, pressured thinking and heightened verbal fluency.
Pyrog..emotional_pattern: Underlying irritability dominates, with deep anxiety and a sinking dread centered around the heart. They are prone to frightful dreams and persistent, intrusive thoughts that prevent sleep, reflecting a pervasive sense of internal decay and restless unease.

Ran-b..personality: A highly sensitive, irritable individual who is easily excitable and prone to quarrelsomeness. They resemble the broken-down state of chronic invalids—weak, tottering, and deeply reactive to environmental changes. There is a marked susceptibility to fright and vexation, with a constitution that has been worn down by prolonged suffering or excess.
Ran-b..mental_state: Confusion of mind predominates, with vertigo triggered by cold air and a peculiar sensation of enlargement of the head. The mental faculties are dulled and besotted, particularly in cases of prolonged alcohol use, with a tendency toward epileptiform episodes and sudden fainting weakness.
Ran-b..emotional_pattern: Deep depression of spirits with an active desire to die characterizes the core emotional state. There is a pronounced fear of ghosts and an underlying irritability that easily escalates into quarrelsomeness. The emotional pattern reflects a person who is both frightened and combative—reacting to their suffering with alternating despair and agitation.

Rhod..personality: A restless, weather-sensitive individual who cannot tolerate stillness and must keep moving to find relief. They are forgetful and easily scattered, losing track of conversations mid-sentence and omitting words in writing. They have an aversion to their own business and are unusually susceptible to the effects of wine.
Rhod..mental_state: The mind is marked by pronounced forgetfulness and cognitive lapses—forgetting what one is saying while speaking, and leaving out words when writing. There is a dread of approaching storms, particularly thunder, revealing a nervous temperament acutely attuned to atmospheric changes. Mental faculties decline during rest and cold, damp conditions.
Rhod..emotional_pattern: The core emotional state is one of anxious restlessness driven by a deep sensitivity to environmental forces, especially storms and cold. There is a pervasive fearfulness tied to weather changes, producing a nervous dread that precedes thunderstorms. An underlying weariness and aversion to responsibilities suggests a person worn down by chronic suffering who withdraws from engagement with daily obligations.

Rhus-t..personality: The Rhus Toxicodendron constitutional type is fundamentally restless and driven, unable to remain still yet quickly exhausted by sustained effort. They are industrious people who must keep moving to feel comfortable, but their energy is limited and they cycle between activity and collapse. There is a deep physical and mental rigidity that loosens with warming up, mirroring their stiff, guarded approach to life.
Rhus-t..mental_state: Mentally, the Rhus type suffers from prostration and inability to sustain mental effort, with a mind that seizes up like their joints and only functions after warming into a task. There is incoherent thinking under stress, anxiety with apprehension especially at night, and a pervasive sense of impending misfortune. Despondency and disgust for life dominate the chronic state, with suicidal ideation yet lacking the courage to act on it.
Rhus-t..emotional_pattern: The core emotional pattern is one of profound sadness and weeping without known cause, coupled with intense irritability and a feeling that some misfortune has occurred. They experience extreme anxiety and nervous restlessness in both acute and chronic states, with fears that intensify markedly at night. There is an ambivalence at their center — they desire death yet fear it, want relief yet cannot rest — reflecting an emotional life trapped between despair and agitation.

Rumx..personality: A chilly, sensitive individual who is highly reactive to environmental changes, particularly cold air and weather shifts. They tend to be sedentary, preferring stillness and regularity, as any irregularity in breathing or motion aggravates their complaints.
Rumx..mental_state: The mental picture is only partially developed, but shows sadness, low-spiritedness, and aversion to work. There is an irritable mental excitability, suggesting a nervous temperament that is easily overstimulated yet lacks the energy or motivation to engage productively.
Rumx..emotional_pattern: The core emotional tone is one of quiet despondency and irritability. The patient is weighed down by a pervasive low mood and feels burdened by effort, retreating into passivity and withdrawal rather than expressing distress outwardly.

Ruta.personality: The Ruta personality is fundamentally irritable, inclined to contradict and quarrel, with a dissatisfied disposition toward both self and others. They are restless individuals who cannot keep still, driven by a nervous restlessness similar to Rhus, yet carrying a deeper chronic weariness from prolonged overexertion.
Ruta.mental_state: The mental symptoms are not strongly differentiated but center on a general dissatisfaction and inclination toward contradiction. There is a melancholy disposition that worsens toward evening, accompanied by mental dejection and a sense of despondency. The mind reflects the same exhaustion seen in the body — a slow, chronic depletion from sustained overuse rather than acute crisis.
Ruta.emotional_pattern: The core emotional pattern is one of low-spirited anxiety combined with irritability, creating a person who is both dejected and combative. There is a deepening melancholy as evening approaches, suggesting emotional reserves that drain over the course of the day. The despondency reflects an inner sense of being worn down and unable to fully recover, mirroring the remedy's physical theme of slow repair and persistent soreness.

Sabad..personality: The Sabadilla patient is a chilly, hypersensitive individual who craves warmth in all forms—hot drinks, hot air, warm wrappings. They are deeply susceptible to environmental stimuli, particularly odors, and even the mere thought of certain triggers can provoke physical symptoms. There is a marked tendency toward nervousness, hysteria, and an unbalanced mental state accompanied by various nervous manifestations.
Sabad..mental_state: The mind is dominated by erroneous impressions and fixed delusions about the body—imagining limbs are crooked, the chin elongated, the body withering or shrunken, or believing oneself pregnant from mere flatus. Mental exertion aggravates symptoms and paradoxically induces sleepiness; the patient falls asleep while meditating or reading. Thinking itself worsens complaints, and there is a stupefying quality to the headaches and mental fog, particularly in feeble, overtaxed children.
Sabad..emotional_pattern: The emotional life is colored by groundless fears and imaginary suffering—the patient believes in horrible diseases and fatal outcomes with no objective basis, yet the distress is more intense than if real pathology existed. There is a hysterical quality with convulsive tremblings and cataleptic episodes, and the patient often receives no sympathy from others because the suffering appears unfounded. Appetite and desire fluctuate between complete aversion and sudden hunger once eating begins, reflecting the same pattern of emotional instability between disgust and engagement.

Sabin..personality: The Sabina constitution is a plethoric, full-blooded individual prone to circulatory turmoil and venous congestion. They are heat-sensitive, wanting open air and windows open, unable to tolerate warm rooms or excess clothing. They tend to build up to a state of apparent robustness only to be broken down by acute hemorrhagic episodes.
Sabin..mental_state: The mental state is characterized by acute suffering and distress, particularly during hemorrhagic or inflammatory crises, with violent pains that cause the patient to cry out. There is a restless urgency driven by intense physical sensations of fullness, throbbing, and burning throughout the body. The mind is dominated by the physical turmoil rather than displaying independent psychological pathology.
Sabin..emotional_pattern: Emotionally, the Sabina patient is reactive and expressive under suffering, screaming out from sudden knife-like shooting pains. There is a pattern of alternating states—periods of apparent recovery and plethoric well-being giving way to exhaustion and anemia after hemorrhagic episodes. The emotional life mirrors the physical cycle of building up and breaking down, with acute distress during crises and relative calm between them.

Sang..personality: A chilly, cold-sensitive individual prone to periodic patterns and routine suffering, often pale-faced with a tendency toward chronic catarrhal conditions. They are deeply affected by environmental changes—drafts, damp weather, seasonal shifts—and are hypersensitive to flowers, odors, and sensory stimuli.
Sang..mental_state: The mental state is characterized by a surface-level engagement with illness rather than deep constitutional disturbance, with anxiety preceding episodes of vomiting and prostration. There is a general mental dullness and withdrawal during acute episodes, particularly the weekly sick headaches that drive the patient to seek darkness, quiet, and stillness.
Sang..emotional_pattern: The emotional pattern centers on a recurring cycle of building distress followed by temporary relief—mirroring the physical pattern of mounting headache resolved by vomiting, or accumulating stomach disorder suddenly discharged by gushing diarrhea. There is a pervasive false hunger or inner emptiness that is not satisfied by external nourishment, with aversion at the thought and smell of food despite a gnawing, sinking faintness.

Sars..personality: A profoundly weakened, prematurely aged constitution marked by sluggishness and deterioration of both mind and body. The Sarsaparilla type is the person who appears decades older than their actual age — enfeebled, tottering, shrivelled — often as a result of chronic disease, excess, or suppressed conditions. They present a picture of deep constitutional breakdown where all organs and tissues have become flabby, congested, and slow.
Sars..mental_state: The mind is dazed, slow, and unable to comprehend, reflecting a weakness that borders on imbecility. There is a persistent dreamlike feeling, as if living in a fog, accompanied by progressive memory loss. Mental prostration parallels the physical decline, with diminished capacity for focused thought or clear reasoning.
Sars..emotional_pattern: A state of deep weariness and resignation pervades the emotional life, with the patient always tired and full of distress, particularly at night. There is an underlying anxiety tied to physical suffering — the child who screams in anticipation of pain exemplifies the dread and helplessness characteristic of this remedy. Financial worry and the burden of chronic illness compound the emotional exhaustion, producing a person who has given up fighting their deterioration.

Sec..personality: The Secale constitutional type is a thin, emaciated, prematurely aged individual with a withered, scrawny appearance and unhealthy skin. They are restless and paradoxical in nature—feeling intensely hot internally while being ice-cold externally, craving open air and wanting to be uncovered despite objective coldness. Their vital force is depleted, lending them a worn-out, dried-up quality that manifests both physically and temperamentally.
Sec..mental_state: The mental state tends toward active mania with great excitement, loss of propriety, and disregard for social norms—modesty is entirely abandoned during acute episodes. Convulsions beginning in the face accompany mental disturbance, and the nervous and mental symptoms characteristically worsen during hemorrhagic episodes. There is a hysterical diathesis with spasmodic, erratic mental functioning that reflects the underlying circulatory and nervous degeneration.
Sec..emotional_pattern: The emotional pattern is dominated by a desperate, self-destructive impulsivity—exemplified by the willingness to risk death rather than endure an unwanted situation, followed by deep regret and chronic suffering. There is profound despair about health, as these patients recognize they have placed themselves beyond help, carrying a lingering sense of damage that cannot be undone. The emotional life is marked by restless agitation, hypersensitivity, and an inability to tolerate confinement or warmth, always seeking relief through exposure and openness.

Sel..personality: A deeply exhausted and depleted individual who has been worn down by excess — whether sexual, alcoholic, or prolonged illness. They are someone whose vital force has been profoundly drained, leaving them unfit for work, dreading company, yet paradoxically becoming excitable and talkative in the evening.
Sel..mental_state: The mind is dull, confused, and extremely forgetful during waking hours, yet remarkably active in sleep where forgotten things are remembered in dreams. Mental exertion is utterly exhausting; they mispronounce words, stammer, fail to understand what they read or hear, and have become incompetent in business affairs. The mind dwells persistently on lascivious thoughts despite physical impotence, creating a painful split between desire and capacity.
Sel..emotional_pattern: Extreme sadness and indifference to surroundings form the emotional baseline, punctuated by irritability — especially after sexual emissions or sleep. There is an irresistible craving for alcoholic stimulants suggesting a pattern of self-medicating emotional pain, and a deep dread of social interaction born not from anxiety but from the sheer exhaustion that human contact demands.

Senec..personality: A pale, weakly, anemic young woman prone to chlorosis ('green sickness') who is constitutionally delicate and sensitive to environmental influences. She is easily affected by cold, damp conditions and drafts of air, reflecting a fragile constitution that struggles to maintain vital functions.
Senec..mental_state: Marked by nervous irritability and lassitude, with a general mental weariness that accompanies the progressive physical decline. The mental state reflects the underlying vital depletion, with diminished energy and a passive susceptibility to illness rather than active mental disturbance.
Senec..emotional_pattern: The emotional pattern centers on a quiet, resigned suffering with deepening fatigue and withdrawal as the constitutional state deteriorates. There is an underlying vulnerability and sensitivity, with the emotional life suppressed much as the menstrual flow is suppressed, the vital force turning inward and expressing through vicarious channels such as hemorrhage and catarrhal discharges.

Seneg..personality: A restless, physically driven individual who cannot tolerate stillness and feels compelled to keep moving despite deep exhaustion. The Senega type endures suffering with a stoic, uncomplaining quality, yet is fundamentally worn down and fragile, often presenting in elderly or depleted constitutions.
Seneg..mental_state: The mind is dominated by a sense of oppression and narrowing, mirroring the physical sensation that the chest is too narrow. There is a persistent, unrelenting quality to their mental experience—a constant irritation that leaves them not a moment's rest, preventing calm or repose.
Seneg..emotional_pattern: The core emotional experience is a fear of suffocation and a deep distress born from the inability to expel what burdens them. There is an underlying anxiety tied to physical vulnerability, a sense of struggling against something tenacious and overwhelming that they cannot quite dislodge or resolve.

Sep..personality: A tall, slim, sallow woman who has become emotionally flat and intellectually dulled, often appearing prematurely aged. She is irritable, obstinate, permits no opposition, and vents spite on those she loves best, yet simultaneously dreads being alone. Her temperament swings unpredictably between yielding gentleness and disagreeable obstinacy.
Sep..mental_state: The mind is sluggish, stupid, and forgetful, with slow thinking and an inability to perform mental labor. She may appear intoxicated or benumbed, answering questions only in monosyllables when pressed, and sits in taciturn melancholy saying nothing. There is fear of insanity, fear of poverty, fear of ghosts, and a sense that unseen figures fill the atmosphere around her.
Sep..emotional_pattern: The most striking feature is the abolition of natural affection — she knows she ought to love her husband and children but feels nothing, as if all capacity for warmth has been stilled. She finds no joy in life, all things seem strange and unreal, and she is worse in company yet dreads solitude. This emotional deadness alternates with spells of weeping, sarcasm, and a compulsion to annoy others and relate her grievances.

Sil..personality: A yielding, mild, and gentle temperament that lacks stamina and firmness, much like a stalk of grain without its silica coating. The patient is timid, retiring, and wants to shirk responsibilities, yet when forced into action can perform with surprising competence and accuracy.
Sil..mental_state: Marked by a profound dread of failure and fear of undertaking any task, despite possessing the actual ability to succeed. The mind is weakened from prolonged mental effort, producing a brain-fag characteristic of professional and studious persons — clergymen, lawyers, students — who have exhausted themselves through sustained intellectual work and sleepless nights.
Sil..emotional_pattern: Deeply anxious and lacking self-confidence, with an underlying irritability and irascibility that emerges when provoked, though the default state is one of tearful mildness and gentle withdrawal. Religious melancholy, sadness, despondency, and a pervasive sense of embarrassment and self-consciousness dominate, with the patient feeling their own selfhood so acutely that they cannot lose themselves in their work or public duties.

Spig..personality: A debilitated, run-down individual who is highly sensitive to cold, atmospheric changes, and all forms of stimulation including noise, light, motion, and even mental exertion. They sit transfixed and still, avoiding movement because any jarring or activity intensifies their suffering.
Spig..mental_state: Weak memory with marked disinclination to work and an inability to concentrate, as even thinking aggravates their complaints. Restless and anxious with solicitude about the future, yet paradoxically may sit motionless to avoid provoking pain. So nervous at times that they feel they must fly, full of excitement, unable to keep still or maintain self-control.
Spig..emotional_pattern: Gloomy with a suicidal mood, easily irritated or offended by the slightest provocation. Harbors peculiar fears, especially of pointed things such as pins, and carries a deep anxiety and apprehension about the future. The intensity of their suffering leaves its mark both physically and emotionally, producing prostration, cold sweat, and a state of nervous exhaustion.

Squil..personality: A chilly, physically sensitive constitution who craves warmth and cannot tolerate the least draft. Not hysterical or emotionally demonstrative; the vital force expresses itself through shifting physical complaints rather than psychological displays.
Squil..mental_state: Kent's lecture reveals remarkably few mental symptoms, suggesting a remedy picture dominated by physical pathology rather than mental disturbance. The patient is not delirious or confused but is burdened by the exhausting cycle of spasmodic coughing, gagging, and involuntary discharges that consume their energy.
Squil..emotional_pattern: Notably lacking the tearfulness of Pulsatilla or the hysteria of Ignatia despite sharing some physical similarities with both. The emotional state is one of quiet, uncomplaining endurance marked by an eagerness for relief — as seen in the child who seizes the cup eagerly yet can only manage small sips.

Stann..personality: A constitutionally weakened individual who has been declining over a long period, becoming increasingly averse to work and activity. They are deeply fatigued, finding all effort irksome, whether business duties or household tasks, with a progressive loss of vitality that shows in their waxy, sallow appearance.
Stann..mental_state: The mind becomes stunned and overwhelmed during intense pain, with diminished capacity for mental exertion. There is a growing inability to engage with responsibilities, not from laziness but from a deep-seated exhaustion that affects both mental and physical functioning.
Stann..emotional_pattern: Marked by a resigned weariness and increasing sensitivity — hypersensitive to pain, to cold, to every change in weather and environment. There is a passive, yielding quality to their suffering; they endure gradually building distress rather than acute crises, mirroring the characteristic Stannum pain pattern of slow crescendo and slow decline.

Staph..personality: A refined, sensitive individual who maintains strict self-control and suppresses emotions rather than expressing them openly. Excitable and easily aroused but rarely manifests anger outwardly, instead turning inward and suffering silently from pent-up feelings. Often intellectually oriented but physically and nervously depleted from sexual excesses or prolonged emotional suppression.
Staph..mental_state: Marked by dullness, brain-fag, and loss of memory, with a characteristic sensation of numbness or woodenness in the head as if the brain were made of wood. Cannot concentrate, makes mistakes in writing and speaking, and struggles with simple mental tasks like arithmetic after episodes of suppressed indignation. The mind dwells excessively on sexual or grievance-related subjects, leading to further nervous exhaustion.
Staph..emotional_pattern: The core pattern is suppressed indignation and pent-up wrath that produces profound physical and mental collapse—trembling, sleeplessness, and prostration. Becomes speechless from anger rather than expressing it, then suffers intensely from the consequences of self-control. Deep sensitivity to insult and injustice combined with hypersensitivity at every level—physical, emotional, and sensory—so that the slightest touch or provocation can shatter composure entirely.

Stram..personality: An intensely violent and turbulent constitution marked by wild, frenzied behavior including cursing, tearing clothes, and erotic displays. The individual oscillates between states of extreme agitation and profound suffering, with a haggard, pallid appearance when not in active delirium.
Stram..mental_state: The mind is in constant uproar with vivid hallucinations of animals, ghosts, devils, and departed spirits, progressing from illusions the patient recognizes as false to fixed delusions they cannot be reasoned out of. Strange distortions of body image arise, with beliefs that the body is ill-shapen, elongated, or deformed. Delirium ranges from bland murmuring to furious raving with attempts to stab and bite, accompanied by incoherent, loquacious speech.
Stram..emotional_pattern: Dominated by extreme fear and anxiety, particularly triggered by darkness, running water, and bright light, with terror so intense that children awaken screaming and clinging to those nearby. A deep undercurrent of religious despair manifests as believing one has sinned beyond redemption, alternating with manic exhortation and prayer. Rage and violent laughter with sardonic expression cycle rapidly with states of melancholic hopelessness and profound suffering.

Sulph..personality: The Sulphur type is the 'ragged philosopher' — a disheveled, sedentary intellectual absorbed in abstract speculation and invention, indifferent to personal appearance, cleanliness, and social convention. He is self-absorbed, ungrateful, and lives in disorder, caring little for the opinions or needs of others while believing himself superior to educated society.
Sulph..mental_state: The mind is marked by philosophical mania — an obsessive, fruitless tracing of causes to their origins, dwelling on unanswerable abstract questions without productive outcome. There is confusion of thought, inability to concentrate, aversion to systematic work, and a dullness upon waking that makes gathering oneself after sleep difficult. The patient may oscillate between inventive fixation and complete mental indolence.
Sulph..emotional_pattern: The core emotional pattern is one of deep selfishness combined with religious melancholy and despair — the patient may sink into moaning prayer, convinced he has sinned beyond redemption, or may become proudly delusional, imagining himself in possession of beautiful things while surrounded by rags. There is a fundamental dissatisfaction and satiety of life, longing for death, too lazy to rouse himself yet too unhappy to live, with startling nervousness and restless, dream-disturbed sleep.

Sul-ac..personality: A deeply exhausted, hurried individual who rushes through everything yet never feels satisfied with the pace of life. Despite profound physical and mental depletion, there is a restless urgency—eating, working, and moving must all be done faster, creating an internal contradiction between weakness and driven impatience.
Sul-ac..mental_state: Marked prostration of mind with irresolution and unwillingness to answer questions. The mental state is characterized by a peculiar combination of extreme hurriedness and inability to concentrate, with a sense that nothing is done quickly or well enough by oneself or others.
Sul-ac..emotional_pattern: Pervading deep sadness with continuous weeping and an inconsolable quality—nothing can be done to please them. Intensely fretful and irritable over the slightest cause, with a sour dissatisfaction toward everyone around them, reflecting the same corrosive, excoriating quality seen throughout the physical symptom picture.

Syph..personality: A deeply weakened, depleted individual who has lost vitality and industriousness, often abandoning responsibilities and withdrawing from active life. They are indifferent to friends and find no delight in anything, presenting as passive, lamenting, and sad.
Syph..mental_state: Marked cognitive decline with forgetfulness for faces, names, dates, and events, along with inability to calculate. There is a persistent sense of depersonalization — always feeling 'not himself' — accompanied by fears of insanity, aphasia, and progressive mental weakness bordering on imbecility.
Syph..emotional_pattern: Dominated by deep melancholia, despair of recovery, and a pervasive dread of both night and morning. Laughing and weeping occur without cause, reflecting emotional instability, while indifference to loved ones and loss of all pleasure signal a profound emotional flattening beneath the surface despair.

Tarent..personality: An intensely restless, hyperkinetic individual driven by constant need for movement—dancing, jumping, running—with marked cunning and deceptiveness, feigning illness and employing fox-like strategies. Deeply reactive to sensory stimuli, particularly music, which can either soothe or violently excite, and strongly averse to vivid colors (green, red, black).
Tarent..mental_state: The mind is dominated by extreme anxiety and agitation, with impaired memory, irritability, and paroxysms of insanity featuring threatening behavior, destructiveness, and violent outbursts including striking others and tearing clothing. Depraved imagination prevails with hallucinations of monsters, insects, strangers, and ghosts, alongside paranoid ideation of being insulted or assaulted, and a refusal to answer when questioned.
Tarent..emotional_pattern: The core emotional landscape alternates between frenzied excitement—singing, dancing, and weeping simultaneously—and deep dementia with profound sadness. Consolation provokes weeping, contradiction triggers anger, and grief and excitement worsen all symptoms. There is a complete loss of shame with uncontrollable lascivious impulses, combined with a desire to withdraw into darkness and solitude when overwhelmed.

Ther..personality: A hypersensitive, nervous constitution marked by hysterical tendencies, oscillating between restless busyness that accomplishes nothing and complete aversion to work. The individual is profoundly reactive to environmental stimuli, particularly noise and motion, to a degree that penetrates the entire body and produces physical illness.
Ther..mental_state: Mental depression and sadness alternate with paradoxical hilarity, joyousness, and singing, especially during headache. There is a characteristic disconnection from the body, such as feeling the vertex does not belong to her, and a confused desire state where the person wants food but cannot identify what. Aversion to work and business reflects deep mental fatigue rather than laziness.
Ther..emotional_pattern: Anxiety, trembling, and a pervasive sense of vulnerability dominate, with fainting from the least exertion revealing emotional and physical fragility. Weeping from pain (especially toothache) and sighing point to a low threshold for emotional overwhelm. The core pattern is one of hysterical sensitivity where emotions rapidly translate into physical symptoms—particularly nausea, vertigo, and chilliness.

Thuj..personality: The Thuja constitutional type is secretive and deceptive by nature, able to control themselves among strangers while showing their true irritable, quarrelsome temperament only to those closest to them. They are fundamentally dishonest in presentation, with a disposition to cheat, and prefer to be alone with their peculiar fixed ideas.
Thuj..mental_state: The Thuja mind is dominated by fixed ideas that cannot be reasoned away — delusions of pregnancy, of animals living inside the body, of being followed, or of someone walking alongside them. There is a striking sensation that soul and body are separated, and a belief that they are made of glass and will break. These mental symptoms intensify as ovarian or reproductive pathology deepens.
Thuj..emotional_pattern: The core emotional pattern is one of violent irritability, intense jealousy, and quarrelsomeness directed primarily at intimate family members — the husband or mother — while maintaining a controlled facade with outsiders. There is a deep desire for isolation and withdrawal, with hysteria emerging under the pressure of physical suffering, particularly during menstruation when pain can drive the patient to cry aloud.

Tub..personality: A restless, cosmopolitan type with a constant desire to travel, change surroundings, and seek something new — always wanting to go somewhere or find a new doctor. Constitutionally debilitated with inherited weakness, easily fatigued, and prone to relapsing conditions that never fully resolve.
Tub..mental_state: Aversion to mental work with persistent, tormenting thoughts that crowd upon each other during the night. Hopelessness pervades many complaints, and the mind may border on insanity — intellectual symptoms and lung symptoms are notably interchangeable in this constitution.
Tub..emotional_pattern: Anxious from evening until midnight, especially during fever, with a sullen, taciturn, and irritable disposition. Weary of life with a deep restlessness that drives the person to move, walk, and travel, as sitting still intensifies both physical and emotional suffering.

Valer..personality: A highly excitable, nervous temperament most often seen in women and children, prone to hysterical and hypochondriacal states. The person is restless and cannot remain still, feeling compelled to move about, with complaints that constantly shift and wander from place to place.
Valer..mental_state: The mind is in a state of extreme activity and tension, rapidly shifting between subjects and ideas with an ecstatic or hysterical quality. There are erroneous perceptions of identity—believing herself to be someone else, seeing imaginary animals and figures, especially at night. Mental symptoms arise during rest and in darkness, clearing when the person walks about.
Valer..emotional_pattern: Deep sadness alternates with irritability and a morose, easily exasperated disposition. There is pronounced fear in the evening and in the dark, with an underlying hypersensitivity to all stimuli. Emotional states are unstable and paroxysmal, flaring during stillness and solitude, and temporarily relieved by motion and activity.

Verat..personality: A restless, driven individual who compulsively needs to stay busy and occupied, often channeling energy into repetitive or purposeless activity. They oscillate between extremes of intense engagement and complete collapse, with a tendency toward religious preoccupation and moralistic fervor.
Verat..mental_state: The mind is marked by violence, destructiveness, and delirium, with a compulsion to tear, cut, and destroy. There is a pronounced tendency toward religious mania—believing oneself divinely chosen, preaching loudly, exhorting others to repentance—alternating with states of silent, brooding melancholia and deep despair. Loquacity and rapid speech characterize the excited phases, while hopelessness and suicidal ideation mark the depressive ones.
Verat..emotional_pattern: The core emotional pattern swings between wild, frenzied states of exaltation and profound, inconsolable grief over real or imagined misfortunes. There is deep-seated fear—of death, of damnation, of cosmic catastrophe—underlying the emotional life, with hysteria, weeping, and desperate screaming giving way to silent brooding and complete emotional withdrawal.

Zinc..personality: A deeply enfeebled, nervous constitution marked by extreme sensitivity to all sensory stimuli—noise, light, conversation—combined with an underlying slowness and sluggishness of all mental and physical functions. The patient appears prematurely aged, pallid, and withered, with a docile temperament that becomes irritable when provoked.
Zinc..mental_state: The mind is slow, weak, and forgetful, with poor memory and a characteristic need to have questions repeated before comprehending and answering them—staring blankly before the face lights up with understanding. There is marked mental prostration and exhaustion, progressing from dullness and stupor toward unconsciousness in severe states, yet with paradoxical startling and hypersensitivity to minor stimuli.
Zinc..emotional_pattern: The core emotional pattern is one of nervous exhaustion manifesting as oversensitivity and irritability—morose when others talk, distressed by conversation, yet fundamentally docile and compliant until pushed. There is a restless, fidgety quality especially in the extremities (notably the feet), reflecting an inner agitation trapped within a depleted system that cannot adequately discharge its tension.
